sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 1 action in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: MOUSE(row=33, col=15).
You are still on the same level.
The game is over.
Current state: step 156, level 2.
Valid actions right now: LEFT, RIGHT, MOUSE, ACTION7.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current knowledge ledger from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE LEDGER.
Pass it as the `world_model` argument of the `python` tool call, alongside `code`. That argument is the one that carries forward, so send it on every call, even when you reply with a tool call and no assistant text.
Game-tier fields (action_semantics, object_taxonomy, hud_map, win_pattern, level_log, cross_level_notes) persist across levels; level-tier fields (world_model, goal_model, recent_findings, open_questions, current_plan, strategy_log, failed_probes) reset when a level ends. Record every unproductive probe in failed_probes so it is never repeated, and tag claims VERIFIED(step N) or HYPOTHESIS.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Knowledge ledger carried from earlier turns:
Game knowledge (persists across levels):
- Action semantics: {'LEFT': 'Moves player left ~6 columns', 'RIGHT': 'Moves player right ~6 columns', 'MOUSE': 'Clicks on position. Clicking green blocks collects them but game over after 8-9 total green clicks. Clicking dots or purple blocks adds 1 purple pixel to bottom bar. Player movement (LEFT/RIGHT) does NOT reset game over counter.', 'ACTION7': "Invalid action - gives 'Unknown action' error despite being in valid_actions list"}
- Object taxonomy: {'background': 'black with gray dots', 'green_blocks': 'light green 21-pixel blocks in 2x7 grid at rows 25-30 and 31-36, cols 13-52', 'purple_blocks_top': '3 purple blocks at rows 7-10, cols 14, 20, 26', 'dots': 'yellow/white dots at row 11 below purple blocks - clicking adds purple pixels to bottom bar', 'player': 'blue+yellow small object', 'platforms': 'large sky blue regions'}
- HUD map: {'bottom_bar': 'purple+white at row 63, resets on game over. Filling to 9 does NOT unlock green collection.'}
- Win pattern: {'level1': 'Collect green blocks with MOUSE, move LEFT toward pink cross to complete level', 'level2': 'UNKNOWN - all attempts to collect 14 green blocks fail with game over after 8-9 clicks'}
- Level log: {'level1': 'Completed in 50 actions - collect greens with MOUSE, approach pink cross, LEFT to complete', 'level2': 'Multiple game overs. All strategies failed: batch clicks, bar filling, interleaving with dots, player movement between clicks. Need fundamentally different approach.'}
- Cross-level notes: Level 2: Game over triggers after 8-9 green block clicks regardless of bar state, dot interleaving, or player movement. ACTION7 is invalid. The solution must involve a mechanic not yet discovered - possibly clicking non-green objects, using the player character differently, or a specific sequence/order.
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it. Tag claims VERIFIED(step N) when the evidence is in `history`, otherwise HYPOTHESIS.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
The `world_model` tool argument is the reliable way to update the ledger. Assistant text is optional; if you write any, keep it short and prefix lines with `Action semantics:`, `Object taxonomy:`, `HUD map:`, `Win pattern:`, `Level log:`, `Cross-level notes:`, `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, or `Failed probes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 2: you have used 105 actions; a strong score needs about 48 or fewer. You are 2.2x over the target.
NET-ZERO WASTE: your last 19 actions returned the board to a state already seen 19 actions ago — that round-trip made no progress yet still cost 19 actions. Do not extend-and-retract or oscillate; a sequence that ends where it began is pure waste.
REVISIT WASTE: this board state has recurred 7 times recently — you are cycling back through states you have already visited. Break the loop and commit to a new line of play.
If you are not steadily making progress toward the level goal, commit to your single best hypothesis and the shortest sequence that tests it — do not exhaustively scan rows/columns or enumerate every option. Test one idea, read the result, then decide.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Game over again after just 1 green click! The game over counter must be persistent across game overs - it's counting ALL green clicks ever made, not just within a single session. This means I've already used up my 8-9 green clicks from previous attempts.
Let me reconsider the problem entirely. The game over counter persists, so I can't collect any more greens. I need a fundamentally different approach.
Looking at the current board:
- 14 green blocks (2 rows of 7) - they reset after game over
- 3 purple blocks at top
- 9 dots (yellow/white) below purple blocks
- Player at bottom
- Bar at bottom with purple pixels
New hypothesis: Maybe the goal isn't to collect green blocks at all. Maybe I need to:
1. Fill the bar completely with purple pixels by clicking dots
2. Or click the purple blocks themselves
3. Or do something with the player character
Wait - let me reconsider. The bar has been accumulating purple pixels across game overs. Currently it has some amount. What if I need to fill the ENTIRE bar (all 64 pixels) with purple?
Actually, let me think about this differently. In level 1, I collected green blocks and moved LEFT toward a pink cross. In level 2, there's no pink cross visible. Maybe the mechanic is completely different.
Let me check: what if I need to click the purple blocks at the top? Or what if I need to move the player to specific positions?
Actually, I just realized something - the game over counter seems to persist across resets. If I can't click greens anymore, I need to find another way. Let me try clicking the purple blocks at the top instead of the dots.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Game over after 1 green click - counter persists across resets
# Can't collect greens anymore. Need completely different approach.
# Try clicking the purple blocks at the top instead

# Check current state
seg = current_frame.segmentation
greens = len([n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N'])
bar_purple = sum(n['pixels'] for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'p')
print(f"Greens: {greens}, Bar purple: {bar_purple}")

# Try clicking the 3 purple blocks at top
# Purple blocks at (7,14), (7,20), (7,26) with 12 pixels each
action([
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 8, 'col': 15},
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 8, 'col': 21},
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 8, 'col': 27}
])
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
{"game_tier": {"action_semantics": {"ACTION7": "Invalid", "LEFT": "Moves player left ~6 columns", "MOUSE": "Green: game over persists across resets. Dots: adds to bar. Purple blocks: untested.", "RIGHT": "Moves player right ~6 columns"}, "cross_level_notes": "Game over counter persists - cannot collect greens anymore. Must find new mechanic.", "hud_map": {"bottom_bar": "Accumulates purple across resets"}, "level_log": {"level2": "Green counter persists across game overs. Trying purple block clicks."}, "win_pattern": {"level1": "Collect green blocks, move LEFT toward pink cross", "level2_hypothesis": "Click purple blocks or fill bar completely"}}, "level_tier": {"current_plan": "Click purple blocks at top to test new mechanic", "failed_probes": ["All green collection strategies - counter persists"], "goal_model": "Find mechanic that doesn't involve green block collection", "recent_findings": ["Game over counter persists across resets - 0 greens left to click"], "world_model": "14 greens, 3 purple blocks, 9 dots, bar accumulating purple"}}
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
